outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Iran Economy, Oil, Gas Prices and Inflation — The War's Economic Toll
outlets.summary: Economic focus on the war's toll — gas at $3.60/gal, Brent ~$100. Treasury waives Russia oil sanctions for 1 month. Analyzes how the conflict is reshaping global energy markets and household budgets.

outlets.name: Time
outlets.headline: How Much Is the Iran War Costing You? Gas, Oil, Electricity and More
outlets.summary: Consumer-focused breakdown of war costs — gas prices, electricity, food inflation. Covers Hormuz blockade impact on household budgets. Treasury Russia sanctions waiver as emergency measure.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Day 14 Live Updates: KC-135 Crew Dead, NATO Intercepts 3rd Missile, Gas at $3.60
outlets.summary: Day 14 liveblog — KC-135 crew all 6 confirmed dead (US toll now 13). NATO intercepts 3rd Iranian missile over Turkey. Hegseth claims 15,000+ targets hit. Gas at $3.60. Michigan synagogue attack.

outlets.name: Fortune
outlets.headline: Trump Changed His Tune on Gas Prices as Iran War Drives Costs Higher
outlets.summary: Examines Trump's shifting rhetoric on gas prices. Pre-war promise of lower costs contrasted with $3.60/gal reality. Treasury Russia sanctions waiver as acknowledgment of supply crisis.

outlets.name: Tucker Carlson (Independent)
outlets.headline: This Is Israel's War, Not America's War
outlets.summary: Major right-wing media shift — Carlson declares the war is Israel's, not America's. Haaretz analysis frames this as the war 'shattering a taboo' and unleashing the American right against Israel.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: KC-135 Crash, Iraq Threats, and Shipping Attacks — Day 14 of the Iran War
outlets.summary: Markets and military focus — KC-135 crew confirmed dead. Continued shipping attacks near Hormuz. Gas at $3.60/gal. Treasury waives Russia oil sanctions for 1 month.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran War Day 14: NATO Intercepts 3rd Missile, Cluster Bombs Hit Israel, Al-Quds Rally Explosion
outlets.summary: Day 14 comprehensive coverage — NATO intercepts 3rd Iranian missile over Turkey near Adana. Explosion at Al-Quds Day march in Tehran kills 1. 11 cluster missiles penetrate Israeli defenses. Araghchi condemns UNESCO silence.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Turkey Says NATO Defenses Intercepted Third Missile From Iran
outlets.summary: Focused coverage of 3rd NATO intercept over Turkish territory near Adana at 3:25 AM. Raises Article 5 concerns. Turkey increasingly caught between Iran and Israel.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: US-Israel-Iran War Day 14: What We Know
outlets.summary: Day 14 roundup — KC-135 crew dead (US toll 13). Netanyahu press conference. Michigan synagogue attack. Teen girl killed running to shelter. Israel defense chief vows to hold Lebanese land.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Day 14 Live: Cluster Missiles Hit Zarzir, 50+ Wounded; Netanyahu Vows to Crush Iran
outlets.summary: Day 14 liveblog — Iranian cluster missile strike on Zarzir wounds 50+. Netanyahu's first press conference: vows to crush Iran, mocks Mojtaba Khamenei. Defense chief declares Israel will hold Lebanese territory until Hezbollah disarms.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Don't Fall for Regime Change Talk — Israel Is Mowing the Lawn in Iran
outlets.summary: Analysis argues regime change in Iran is not achievable and Israel is repeating a familiar pattern of degrading capabilities without strategic endgame. Frames lowered expectations after initial maximalist rhetoric.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: 11 Iranian Cluster Missiles Penetrated — One Dropped 70 Bombs Over Central Israel
outlets.summary: Detailed analysis of how 11 Iranian cluster missiles bypassed Arrow and David's Sling defenses. One warhead dispersed approximately 70 bomblets. 2 Israelis killed, teen girl killed running to shelter.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran's Missile Blitzkrieg Penetrates Israeli Defenses — 11 Cluster Missiles Break Through
outlets.summary: Frames cluster missile penetration as decisive military achievement. Claims Israeli air defenses are failing. Covers Larijani's declaration that war won't end until enemies are 'sorry.' Pentagon estimated $11.3B in first 6 days.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera (Mojtaba Coverage)
outlets.headline: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Statement as Supreme Leader Amid War
outlets.summary: Coverage of Mojtaba Khamenei's first public statement — vows Hormuz stays closed, calls for closing all US bases. His physical condition remains uncertain. Hegseth claims he is 'likely disfigured.'

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Larijani: War Will Not End Before Enemies Are Sorry
outlets.summary: State agency highlights Larijani's hardline stance rejecting any peace before full accountability. Covers Al-Quds Day rally explosion as evidence of enemy sabotage. UNESCO silence on Iranian cultural sites condemned.
